title: quercus shennongii
intro: Quercus shennongii, also known as the "Chinese corkbark oak," is a plant species in the beech family (Fagaceae) native to China. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly its ability to lower cholesterol levels and reduce inflammation due to its high content of flavonoids and phenolic acids. Quercus shennongii has been traditionally used to promote cardiovascular health, lower blood pressure, and alleviate symptoms of diabetes. Herbal preparations of Quercus shennongii can be found in various forms, including capsules, tablets, and tea, and are often used in combination with other herbs to enhance their therapeutic effects.
